meaning: n.栖木,人所居的高位,有利的地位,杆,河鲈v.(使)栖息,就位,位于
examples: A lady was walking to work when she saw a parrot on a perch in front of a pet store.
The mother carried each baby up a rainforest tree to a safe perch inside a bromeliad plant.
"Very well," answered the sparrow, "do so, and in the meantime, I will perch upon that bush."
For those newcomers who have not yet found their perch, it is only a matter of time.
In the prosaic world of real estate marketing Mark Segal and Jeffrey Jones have found a rarefied perch.
